findings.description: State flag image is moved and effectively isolated from the title content.
findings.evidence: On page 1 of the PDF, the North Carolina flag appears centered above the title slide text. In the HTML, the flag is placed alone near the top-left with a large empty area before the title/content appears later, breaking the intended title-slide composition.

findings.description: Title slide layout is materially altered with excessive whitespace and left-aligned content instead of centered slide composition.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 1 is a centered title slide with flag, title, subtitle, and date grouped together. The HTML separates these elements vertically and left-aligns them, changing the overall structure though all content remains present.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy and emphasis are reduced on the legislation slide.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF uses a large centered title and bold numbering/item labels with clear visual distinction. The HTML renders the same content in smaller, more uniform text, reducing heading prominence and list emphasis.

findings.description: Centered slide layout is converted to a simple left-aligned document flow.
findings.evidence: Page 2 in the PDF is a slide with centered title and carefully spaced numbered bullets. In the HTML, the content becomes a left-aligned flowing text block, preserving order but not the original slide structure.

findings.description: Bullet hierarchy and title prominence are reduced on the river basin modeling slide.
findings.evidence: On page 3, the PDF shows a large centered title and clearly indented multilevel bullets. The HTML keeps the bullets but with smaller text and weaker visual contrast between title, top-level bullets, and sub-bullets.

findings.description: Key definitions slide loses large-title presentation and bullet emphasis.
findings.evidence: Page 4 in the PDF has a prominent centered title and large bullet list. The HTML preserves the list items but presents them in a smaller, plain left-aligned format with less distinction.

findings.description: Stakeholder slide formatting is weakened, with reduced title prominence and lighter list hierarchy.
findings.evidence: On page 5, the PDF has a large centered title and strongly differentiated bullet levels. The HTML retains the same content and order but uses a more compact left-aligned text style, making sections less visually distinct.

findings.description: SAB slide formatting loses strong slide-style emphasis.
findings.evidence: Page 6 PDF presents a large centered heading and large bullets/sub-bullets. The HTML preserves the content as a bulleted list but with substantially smaller text and reduced heading/body contrast.

findings.description: Advisory statement slide has reduced visual distinction between title and body text.
findings.evidence: On page 7, the PDF uses a large centered title and oversized bullet text for the advisory statement. The HTML keeps the sentence content intact but appears as smaller left-aligned list text with less emphasis.

findings.description: Planning-purpose slide loses original slide-style typography and emphasis.
findings.evidence: Page 8 in the PDF uses a large centered title and two large bullets. In the HTML, the same content appears as ordinary left-aligned bulleted text with reduced visual hierarchy.

findings.description: Negative-definition slide has diminished formatting hierarchy.
findings.evidence: On page 9, the PDF presents a large centered title and bold, spacious bullets. The HTML retains the three bullet points but in a smaller, flatter left-aligned layout.

findings.description: Nested oval planning diagram is lost in HTML.
findings.evidence: Page 10 PDF contains a key graphic with two nested colored ovals labeled 'Water Supply Planning' and 'River Basin Modeling'. The HTML shows only the text labels and omits the diagram entirely, removing the visual relationship between concepts.

findings.description: Diagram slide structure is simplified into plain text plus later contact section.
findings.evidence: On page 10, the PDF is primarily a diagram slide with a centered title. The HTML reduces it to separate text lines without the enclosing shapes, altering the page structure though labels remain readable.

findings.description: DWR logo is smaller and repositioned relative to the contact information.
findings.evidence: On page 11, the PDF places the DWR logo prominently beneath the centered contact block. In the HTML, the logo is present but smaller and shifted toward the upper-left portion of the visible area relative to the contact text.

findings.description: Contact slide layout changes from centered composition to left-aligned text flow.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 11 is a centered closing slide with title, contact details, and logo grouped in the middle. The HTML presents the content as left-aligned flowing text with the logo separated below, changing the overall visual structure.

findings.description: One sentence contains a transcription error that changes the text from the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF slide 'SAB – What It Is' reads: 'The SAB will advise DENR on an approach to characterize the ecology of river basins and a method the determine the flows necessary for ecological integrity.' HTML contains the same erroneous wording, which appears to mirror the slide text; however, the issue is that the phrase is likely intended to be 'method to determine'. Because the HTML matches the visible PDF text, this is not a conversion fidelity error.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is somewhat flattened: most slide titles are encoded as h2 rather than preserving a richer structural hierarchy for a multi-slide document.
findings.evidence: HTML uses one h1 for 'HB 1743 & Why We Are Here' and h2 for all subsequent slide titles such as 'Major Water-Related Legislation 2010 Ratified Bills', 'Improve River Basin Modeling', and 'Contact Info'. This is acceptable but somewhat simplistic.
